title: Update attack path diagram icons to match file type names

requirements.summary: Replace seven attack path diagram icons with uploaded images matching file type semantics
acceptanceCriteria: Icon at div[12]/img[1] uses word.png for Word document icon
Icon at div[13]/img[1] uses pdf.png for PDF document icon
Icon at div[14]/img[1] uses excel.png for Excel/CSV document icon
Icon at div[15]/img[1] uses script-1.png for script file icon
Icon at div[16]/img[1] uses zip.png for archive file icon
Icon at div[17]/img[1] uses dll-1.png for DLL/library file icon
Icon at div[18]/img[1] uses ppt.png for PowerPoint document icon
All seven uploaded images are correctly mapped to their respective IconType values in AttackPathIcon.tsx

file_operations.description: Update the icon mapping logic to use the uploaded images (word.png, pdf.png, excel.png, script-1.png, zip.png, dll-1.png, ppt.png) from frontend/public/assets/ for their corresponding IconType values. Map word.png to Word document type, pdf.png to PDF type, excel.png to Excel/CSV type, script-1.png to script file type, zip.png to archive type, dll-1.png to DLL/library type, and ppt.png to PowerPoint type. Preserve all existing drag-and-drop functionality and ensure the component continues to handle icon placement and interaction correctly.
